preamble: Nasalization of [g] to [n] typically occurs through assimilation in connected speech or through the historical reduction of nasal-stop clusters.

examples.note: In some Bantu languages, the voiced velar [g] in prenasalized clusters assimilates fully to the nasal, yielding [n] or [ŋ]. See also Meinhof's Law contexts.
Flagged: this shows g→∅ (initial cluster deletion), not g→n. The [g] was dropped, not converted to [n].

sources: Crystal, D. (2008). A Dictionary of Linguistics and Phonetics (6th ed.). Blackwell Publishing.
